title: Review Medication Review contradiction invalidation candidates
description:
Review split-score contradiction exemplars after the April 22 rubric/code promotion and determine which older feedback items should be invalidated under the current Medication Review rubric.

In scope:
- Medication Review: Prescriber
- Medication Review: Dosage
- Medication Review: Pharmacy
- Medication Review: Individual Confirmation

Focus:
- older labels that require all medications instead of most
- older labels that count excluded/discontinued/not-current meds
- older labels that apply SRX rules to SPM calls
- pharmacy labels that accept vague or system-only pharmacy verification despite the published current-pharmacy rubric

Do not invalidate automatically; prepare and review candidate lists first.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Rerunning the Medication Review: Prescriber contradictions report after the first approved invalidation batch and comparing it to the most recent pre-invalidation contradictions report. Goal: verify how the contradiction counts and exemplar set changed after invalidating the old all-meds Prescriber labels.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Reran Medication Review: Prescriber contradictions after the first approved invalidation batch and compared the fresh report against the latest true pre-invalidation report. Fresh post-invalidation report: 8d2253ea-f590-414e-9ede-b01aa11b9ac8 created 2026-04-22 18:50 UTC. Latest pre-invalidation baseline: b509fc02-1b39-45b0-a59f-e868a7319566 created 2026-04-22 18:11 UTC. Before: 54 analyzed, 10 contradictions, 44 aligned. After: 49 analyzed, 7 contradictions, 42 aligned. The five invalidated Prescriber items are fully absent from the fresh contradiction set: 306181806, 305252079, 305141717, 305262577, 305252085. Remaining contradiction set is now: 305141718, 305879339, 305881523, 305930388, 307498132, 307516904, 309504278. Net effect: contradiction rate dropped from 10/54 (18.5 percent) to 7/49 (14.3 percent). Two contradictions surfaced in the fresh selected set that were not in the 18:11 report exemplar list: 305141718 and 309504278. This appears to be normal post-invalidation resurfacing from the remaining eligible pool rather than a failure of the invalidation batch.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Ran post-fix feedback evaluation for Medication Review: Prescriber (eval 7ccfb878-ef0c-43f7-ac4d-5ef326951a10) and verified invalidated feedback IDs are excluded (intersection count 0 against the 5 invalidated IDs).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Prescriber optimizer relaunched successfully via direct CLI using /Users/ryan/miniconda3/bin/python after earlier SCORE_NOT_FOUND failure on explicit start_version pull. New procedure: 9029e3b7-1890-4e38-8909-330337b3803f. Also started Medication Review: Dosage contradiction triage with fresh 180-day contradictions report + eval 30533ae5-eae0-4c96-8917-ebe457d2b6e5. Clean initial invalidation shortlist is currently: a2032807-0f2a-431a-878e-116af9c82bfe, a3a6640c-f1fe-46ae-ad17-1caa662ddf42, 8b777a9b-29ff-448a-ae57-b19978ee30c5, 22ce6344-d998-43a3-90f4-0030f24f5f47. Important separate finding: Dosage champion still has parse_from_start=true and guidelines missing, and several false negatives appear to be parser/config artifacts rather than contradictory labels.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Continuing with Dosage before launching its optimizer. Rationale: post-invalidation contradiction count improved (14 -> 9), but the live Dosage champion still shows a real config problem (parse_from_start=true and missing published guidelines on the champion version), so optimization would currently learn from parser/config noise. Next step is to fix Dosage code+guidelines together, verify via evaluation, then start the Dosage optimizer on the cleaned signal.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Prescriber optimizer startup bug fixed via CLI score-pull resolver patch. Current clean run: procedure 75231fc3-823a-4654-8192-ecc016f6b8d1. Also updated RCA handling to avoid false missing-context clustering and documented conservative contradiction-driven invalidation workflow in skills/plexus-score-optimizer/SKILL.md.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Starting new contradictions-first recovery pass for Medication Review: Prescriber. Scope for this pass:
- use procedure 695a6bcd-f17c-4d04-ae68-358a5e2cfe0b and stalled follow-up 26d26b35-3f96-434e-b7cd-9f14cb7dead0 as primary evidence
- freeze and extract exact contradiction candidates with transcript/edit-comment evidence
- do not invalidate anything without explicit approval of exact IDs
- after approval, build a vetted deterministic regression slice
- restart optimizer from version 63892e5d-bacc-483c-89ff-429ff2131100, not champion, with an explicit hint preferring GPT 5.4 mini and forbidding GPT 5.4 nano/full-context reset/extractor-ledger retries
Current step: collect the operator packet and candidate groups for approval.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Pulled a fresh 90-day Prescriber contradictions block for contradictions-first triage. Current result: 47 eligible items analyzed, 10 contradictions, 37 aligned. No invalidation performed. Next step: extract exact candidate groups, identify likely true contradictions vs policy-gap/implementation cases, and prepare SME-facing clarification questions before requesting approval on any exact IDs.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Fresh 90-day Prescriber contradictions packet completed from report fab5a6a8-8304-417f-9ea9-3ff5e4a9391b. Exact contradiction set recovered from the persisted ReportBlock attachment. Clean invalidation candidates pending user approval: 309504453 (f859ce53-0907-4160-8746-6e73a58f3cc0), 309504386 (868edab3-f263-4598-aee1-3043786875e6), 309504413 (43709332-f4e0-4338-b3e1-e59121b47c92), 308538589 (d8f59b46-3af4-41f6-9130-71779062d65c), 305262247 (d4977d12-1b3e-4e97-9f6e-a3bed718244a), 305141716 (73c423c3-1ac0-4400-bcfe-82db678665b1). Common pattern: reviewer left final Yes/agree even though the score explanation itself says multiple current medications remained unverified and ends in No. Hold for explicit approval only; no invalidation performed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Preparing another Medication Review: Prescriber contradictions run after the two approved rule clarifications were implemented in candidate version ba041b6a-8786-4f31-b082-31b5f999b0e7. First checking whether the FeedbackContradictions block can target a non-champion score version so the rerun reflects the updated guidelines rather than the still-champion older version.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Confirmed from plexus/reports/blocks/feedback_contradictions.py that the FeedbackContradictions block currently fetches guidelines from the score championVersionId only. It does not accept a score_version_id override, so a rerun after non-champion guideline tweaks will still analyze against the current champion rule set. Running a fresh 90-day Prescriber contradictions report anyway for comparability and documenting that limitation explicitly.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Reran Medication Review: Prescriber contradictions via direct CLI. Fresh report: 66a18447-765c-4fdc-a007-74ac220b5d0b (block 6aa7691b-abfe-40fa-89ea-80f75118bfa7). Result: 47 analyzed, 7 contradictions, 40 aligned, 5 topic clusters. Important implementation note: despite the known champion-only fetch path in FeedbackContradictions, the stored output guidelines on this run include both approved clarifications (prescribed-by recognition and explicit at-most-one-miss language). Need to reconcile that with the separate score-info tool still reporting the older championVersionId before assuming promotion state.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting the Prescriber triangulation pass requested by the user. Goal: combine three evidence streams for Medication Review: Prescriber — (1) the fresh contradictions report, (2) mistakes from the latest 180-day feedback evaluation(s), and (3) repeat-offender / recurring issue evidence from the latest optimizer procedures — to identify clean invalidation candidates, gray-zone review items, and any contradictions that are corroborated by multiple analysis types.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Completed Prescriber triangulation across three sources: latest contradictions report 66a18447-765c-4fdc-a007-74ac220b5d0b / block 6aa7691b-abfe-40fa-89ea-80f75118bfa7, latest 180-day feedback evaluation 4e5cbf7b-060b-4c5b-89f9-59c224f8a6b8, and optimizer recurrence from procedures 695a6bcd-f17c-4d04-ae68-358a5e2cfe0b plus 26d26b35-3f96-434e-b7cd-9f14cb7dead0. Overlap summary: all three sources = 308538589 and 309504448; contradiction+evaluation only = 305141716 and 309504413; contradiction+recurrence only = 309504386 and 309504453; evaluation+recurrence only = 305262247, 306169979, 308889128. Important navigation result: 309504386, 309504453, and previously disputed 307498132 are correct on the latest candidate evaluation (predicted Yes / actual yes), so they now look like stale champion/report-path contradictions rather than invalidation targets. The strongest current invalidation review items are 305141716 and 309504413 because both still appear as contradiction exemplars and current-eval errors under the approved rubric. The all-three items 308538589 and 309504448 remain gray-zone because the contradiction signal says the review label looks wrong, but both optimizer recurrence and eval RCA repeatedly attribute them to RelevantWindowsTranscriptFilter preprocessing loss; do not invalidate those first. Evaluation+recurrence-only items 305262247, 306169979, and 308889128 look like real score/configuration or hard borderline cases to keep in the vetted regression slice, not contradiction cleanup. Net: remaining work is mostly score/preprocessing navigation, not open SME policy on the already-settled Prescriber thresholds.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: User approved invalidating the two triangulation-backed Prescriber contradiction items only: 305141716 (73c423c3-1ac0-4400-bcfe-82db678665b1) and 309504413 (43709332-f4e0-4338-b3e1-e59121b47c92). Rationale: both remain contradiction exemplars and current-evaluation errors under the approved rubric. Proceeding with exact-item invalidation now; gray-zone preprocessing cases remain excluded.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Invalidated exactly the two approved Prescriber contradiction items via targeted feedback invalidation: 305141716 (73c423c3-1ac0-4400-bcfe-82db678665b1) and 309504413 (43709332-f4e0-4338-b3e1-e59121b47c92). Both returned updated=true and already_invalid=false. No other Prescriber feedback items were touched.
